manifesto.footnote: A short list of things I believe, after eight years of writing code that has to survive contact with users.

pulse.label: Currently
pulse.title: Building MogHouse — a home for FFXIV players.

aboutPeek.title: Curious by default.
paragraphs: I'm Andrea — a Full Stack developer who treats software like a craft. I care about the small details: a button that feels right, a query that doesn't slow at 100k rows, a UI that respects the user's time.
I work mainly with TypeScript on the web, but move comfortably across the stack. My approach is shaped by editorial design and by the bushidō — the way of disciplined improvement.
aboutPeek.valuesLabel: Three things I keep in mind

values.title: Craft over speed
values.body: Slow work compounds. Rushed foundations are a tax someone always pays later.

values.title: Clarity over cleverness
values.body: Readable code is kinder to your future self. Abstractions earn their place.

values.title: Respect the user
values.body: Their time, bandwidth and attention — none should be an afterthought.

featuredWork.intro: Quality over quantity. End-to-end products — design, build, deploy.

journeyPeek.title: Where I've been.
journeyPeek.intro: A short look at the path so far.

finalCta.title: We should probably talk.
finalCta.lead: Whether it's a role, a project, or just a thoughtful conversation about software — I'd love to hear from you.

about.title: Curious by default.
about.lead: I'm Andrea — a Full Stack developer who treats software like a craft. I care about the small details: a button that feels right, a query that doesn't slow at 100k rows, a UI that respects the user's time.
body: I work mainly with TypeScript on the web — Next.js, React, Node — but I move comfortably across the stack: Postgres, Go for performance-critical pieces, Python for data and ML glue.

My approach is shaped equally by editorial design and by the bushidō — the way of disciplined improvement. I prefer one well-made thing over five rushed ones. Boring fundamentals over flashy abstractions. Production lessons over hype.
Outside the editor I read, train iaidō (slowly), and travel — Japan, mostly, where the obsession with craft feels like home.

items.title: Craft over speed
items.body: Slower, more deliberate work compounds. Shipping fast on a rotten foundation is a tax someone always pays later.

items.title: Clarity over cleverness
items.body: Readable code is kinder to your future self and to the next person who walks in. Abstractions earn their place.

items.title: Respect the user
items.body: Their time, their bandwidth, their attention, their accessibility. None of those should be afterthoughts.

about.skillsIntro: Comfortable across the stack. Honest about what I'm fluent in and what I'm still learning.

work.title: Things I've shipped.
work.lead: A short, deliberate selection — quality over quantity. Most projects are end-to-end: product, design, build, deploy.

journey.title: The path so far.
journey.lead: Roles, milestones and turning points. No fluff.

contact.title: Let's build something.
contact.lead: I'm open to interesting roles, freelance work, and good conversations about software, design, and the occasional kanji.
contact.responseTime: I usually reply within a day or two.

footer.made: Designed and built with care in Italy.
footer.colophon: Set in Fraunces, Geist Mono, Noto Serif JP. Motion by GSAP and Lenis.
